title: Slovakian Pork Dumplings
description: A hearty Slovakian dish featuring flavorful pork dumplings served in a rich, aerated broth.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, black pepper, paprika, and salt.
Gradually add water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small pieces and roll each into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape.
In a large skillet, cook chopped onion and minced garlic in a little oil until softened.
Add cooked pork, caraway seeds, and a pinch of salt to the skillet and stir to combine.
Place a tablespoon of the pork mixture onto the center of each dumpling disk.
Fold the dough over the filling and press edges together to seal.
In a large pot of boiling, salted water, cook the dumplings for 10-12 minutes or until they float.
Serve the dumplings in an aerated broth, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
